candidates.evidence_quote: In recent years, our operating results were impacted by geopolitical and macroeconomic events, causing supply chain challenges and significantly increased commodity and wage inflation.

The impact of ongoing geopolitical and macroeconomic events, including evolving government policies and global trade and tariff dynamics, could lead to further wage inflation, product and services cost inflation
including evolving government policies and global trade and tariff dynamics, could lead to further wage inflation, product and services cost inflation, disruptions in the supply chain
causing supply chain challenges and significantly increased commodity and wage inflation
could lead to further wage inflation, product and services cost inflation, disruptions in the supply chain, staffing challenges, shifts in consumer behavior, and delays in new restaurant openings
which supports our plan for approximately 20% average annual unit growth
our philosophy is to use price increases to help offset key operating cost increases in a manner that balances supporting both our margin objectives and customer traffic levels
grand opening publicity and other customer awareness activities that generate higher than usual customer traffic

We offer all items on our menu, except alcoholic beverages where disallowed by regulation, for off-premise consumption, sales of which comprised approximately 21% of The Cheesecake Factory restaurant sales during fiscal 2025.
During fiscal 2025, alcoholic beverage sales represented 11% of The Cheesecake Factory restaurant sales.
Our brand identity and reputation for offering premium desserts results in a significant level of dessert sales, representing approximately 17% of The Cheesecake Factory sales during fiscal 2025.
The average check for each customer, including beverages and desserts, was approximately $31.79 during fiscal 2025.
Fluctuations in both average sales per location and average sales per productive square foot for fiscal 2025 generally tracked with comparable restaurant sales trends.

Average sales per productive square foot (defined as all interior square footage plus seasonally adjusted exterior patio square footage) for restaurants open for the full year were approximately $1,151 for fiscal 2025.
Restaurant-level preopening costs for a typical location in an established market average approximately $1.0 million to $1.5 million
Total construction costs to build our restaurant premises average approximately $1,100 to $1,200 per interior square foot.
While we continue to operate in a competitive labor environment, we believe our people practices contribute significantly to our ability to attract talent
our people practices contribute significantly to our ability to attract talent and to The Cheesecake Factory restaurants' historically industry-leading retention rates

Our licensees invest their capital to build and operate these restaurants, and we receive initial development fees, site and design fees and ongoing royalties based on our licensees' restaurant sales.
we leverage opportunities in the consumer packaged goods channel by partnering with third-party manufacturers to offer products marketed under The Cheesecake Factory At Home ® mark
Adverse weather conditions and natural disasters may further exacerbate a number of these factors.

notes: Single event: 10-K part 1 of 6 (Item 1 Business). No fiscal_kpis on this chunk. All candidates mined from narrative prose.

Skipped vague/too-broad items: generic 'restaurant industry is highly competitive' (no specific competitive-cost driver), 'experiential dining'/brand-positioning prose, and cybersecurity (mentioned as program oversight, not a source-grounded causal claim with a concrete breach/incident).
menu_pricing and restaurant_traffic split from the same sentence per R2 (two independent causal variables); same_store_sales kept whole per R6 from 'comparable restaurant sales'.

Brand-level facts (North Italia, Flower Child off-premise/check) folded into company-wide drivers since the company-wide form was also evidenced; did not coin separate brand drivers to avoid single-brand overfit where company-wide evidence exists.
